title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
description: The Dursleys, obsessed with normalcy, are increasingly disturbed by strange occurrences and whispers of the Potters, whom they despise. Meanwhile, Albus Dumbledore and Minerva McGonagall arrive on Privet Drive, revealing the death of Lily and James Potter and the miraculous survival of their son, Harry, at the hands of Voldemort. They prepare to leave baby Harry on the Dursleys' doorstep, setting the stage for his unusual upbringing.

nodes.content: The presence of strangely dressed people in cloaks indicates an unusual event.
He overhears whispers about 'The Potters' and 'their son, Harry' from the cloaked people.
He connects the owls, shooting stars, and cloaked people to the 'Potters' and 'her crowd'.
The possibility of being associated with the Potters and their 'kind' fills him with dread.
He doesn't understand the term 'Muggle' or the significance of the strange events.
He believes Harry is responsible for strange occurrences, like the vanishing glass, and fears the implications.
He attempts to deceive Harry about the letters, claiming they are mistakes and that he burned them.
He believes that by physically blocking mail delivery, the senders will give up.
He mutters about 'shaking them off' when driving erratically, believing they are being followed.
He decides to take the family to a remote shack to escape the letters.
He is terrified by Hagrid's magical abilities and the revelation of Harry's wizard identity.
His long-standing deception about Harry's parents and the magical world is exposed.
He believes magic is 'rubbish' and 'magic tricks' taught by a 'crackpot old fool'.
He is now so scared of Harry that he avoids him.
He attempts to mislead Harry about Platform Nine and Three-Quarters, hoping he will be stranded.
He reveals Dudley is having his pig's tail removed at the hospital.
He is still purple-faced and furious at Harry carrying an owl in a cage in public.
Her day was 'nice, normal', contrasting with the strange events Mr. Dursley witnessed.
She confirms her nephew's name is Harry, but dismisses it as 'nasty, common'.
She believes Harry is responsible for strange occurrences, like the vanishing glass, and fears the implications.
She lied to Harry about his parents dying in a car crash to hide the truth of their magical deaths.
She observes the address on Harry's letter and questions how 'they' knew where he sleeps.
She expresses concern that 'they' might be watching the house.
She reminds Uncle Vernon of their oath to 'stamp out that dangerous nonsense' when they took Harry in.
She doubts Uncle Vernon's strategy of blocking mail delivery will work.
She is horrified by Hagrid's presence and the revelation of Harry's wizard identity.
She reveals her long-held resentment and jealousy towards her sister, Lily, for being a witch.
Her long-standing deception about Harry's parents and the magical world is exposed.
She is now so scared of Harry that she avoids him.
She looks terrified at the sight of Harry.
He observes Harry's interaction with the snake and the subsequent vanishing of the glass.
He exaggerates the snake's actions to further incriminate Harry.
He observes Harry receiving a letter and demands to see it.
He is amazed that someone wants to talk to Harry 'this badly'.
He complains about missing television and computer games during their escape.
He is terrified by Hagrid's imposing presence and magical actions.
He experiences a magical transformation into having a pig's tail.
He is now so scared of Harry he won't stay in the same room.
He is going to the hospital to have his pig's tail removed.
He looks terrified at the sight of Harry.
He observes the Dursleys' favoritism towards Dudley and their disdain for him.
He has a recurring dream about a flying motorcycle, a faint memory of his arrival at Privet Drive.
He recognizes that strange things often happen around him, which he cannot explain.
He discovers he can understand and communicate with a boa constrictor.
He recalls a blinding flash of green light and a burning pain on his forehead, which he attributes to the car crash.
He notices that certain strangers in violet or purple clothing seem to recognize and acknowledge him.
He realizes he has never received a letter before and is deeply curious about its sender.
He feels a sense of belonging and importance from receiving a letter addressed specifically to him, even to his cupboard.
He devises a plan to intercept future letters after the first one is taken.
He realizes his eleventh birthday is approaching.
He learns he is a wizard.
He learns the truth about his parents' magical abilities and their deaths at Voldemort's hands.
He is introduced to the concept of Hogwarts, Muggles, and the wizarding world.
He learns he is famous in the wizarding world for surviving Voldemort's attack.
He realizes past strange occurrences were unconscious magic.
He accepts his identity as a wizard despite initial disbelief.
He learns his parents left him a fortune at Gringotts, the wizarding bank.
He learns about Galleons, Sickles, and Knuts.
He learns Gringotts is hundreds of miles under London and about Diagon Alley.
He learns about the Ministry of Magic and its role in concealing magic from Muggles.
He observes Hagrid's difficulty navigating the Muggle world and the Muggles' reactions to Hagrid.
He experiences widespread recognition and admiration from the wizarding community at the Leaky Cauldron.
He observes Professor Quirrell's nervous demeanor and learns he will be a Hogwarts teacher.
He is introduced to Diagon Alley, its shops, and various magical items.
He learns about goblins running Gringotts and the possibility of dragons guarding vaults.
He sees his vast inheritance of gold, silver, and bronze coins in his Gringotts vault.
He learns about the properties of wands, their cores (unicorn hair, phoenix feather, dragon heartstring), and that 'the wand chooses the wizard'.
He learns his wand's core (phoenix feather) came from the same phoenix that provided a feather for Voldemort's wand.
He encounters a prejudiced pure-blood boy who expresses disdain for Muggle-borns.
He learns about Quidditch, the wizarding sport played on broomsticks.
He learns about the four Hogwarts Houses (Slytherin, Hufflepuff) and their reputations.
He learns that magic is not to be used in the Muggle world except in special circumstances.
He receives a snowy owl as a birthday present from Hagrid.
He observes the Dursleys' fear of him and their avoidance.
He learns about Platform Nine and Three-Quarters from his ticket.
He observes the Dursleys' malicious glee at his apparent predicament at King's Cross.
He learns about the hidden entrance to Platform Nine and Three-Quarters by observing the Weasley family.
He observes the bustling, magical atmosphere of Platform Nine and Three-Quarters.
He is recognized as Harry Potter by Fred and George Weasley.
He observes the Weasley family's close-knit, boisterous dynamic.
He reveals to Ron that he only remembers a green light from Voldemort's attack.
He observes Ron's family's financial struggles and his hand-me-down possessions.
He learns about various magical candies and treats, including Bertie Bott's Every Flavor Beans and Chocolate Frogs.
He learns about Famous Witches and Wizards cards found in Chocolate Frogs.
He learns that pictures in the wizarding world move.
He learns about the different flavors of Bertie Bott's Every Flavor Beans.
He observes Hermione Granger's studious nature and bossy demeanor.
He learns he is famous enough to be in wizarding history books.
He learns about the Hogwarts Houses and their reputations, including Slytherin's connection to Voldemort.
He learns about a recent attempted robbery at Gringotts.
He observes Draco Malfoy's continued prejudice and arrogance, and his association with Crabbe and Goyle.
He observes Ron's loyalty and bravery in defending him against Malfoy.
He observes Scabbers' unexpected defensive action against Goyle.
He observes the changing landscape as the train approaches Hogwarts.
He sees Hogwarts Castle for the first time, perched above a great black lake.
He learns that first-year students travel to Hogwarts by boat across the lake.
He observes Professor McGonagall's stern demeanor and realizes she is not to be crossed.
He learns about the Hogwarts Houses (Gryffindor, Hufflepuff, Ravenclaw, Slytherin), their values, and the House Cup competition.
He fears the Sorting Ceremony will involve a difficult magical test he cannot pass.
He observes the fear and nervousness of his fellow first-years regarding the Sorting.
He observes Hermione Granger's studious and bossy nature, even in moments of high tension.
He learns about ghosts inhabiting Hogwarts and their general characteristics.
He learns the Great Hall ceiling is bewitched to look like the sky outside.
He learns the Sorting Hat can read minds and discern a student's character and potential.
He strongly desires not to be sorted into Slytherin.
He experiences widespread recognition and whispers about his identity during the Sorting Ceremony.
He learns about the Gryffindor common room and dormitories.
He observes Dumbledore's eccentric but brilliant personality.
He learns about the magical properties of Hogwarts food, including its ability to appear and disappear.
He learns about the Gryffindor house ghost, Sir Nicholas de Mimsy-Porpington, also known as Nearly Headless Nick.
He learns about the Bloody Baron, the Slytherin house ghost.
He observes the Slytherin students and their house ghost, finding them unpleasant.
He learns about Seamus Finnigan's half-Muggle heritage.
He learns about Neville Longbottom's family's struggle to confirm his magical abilities.
He observes Professor Snape's hostile gaze and feels a sharp pain in his scar.
He learns Professor Snape teaches Potions and desires Professor Quirrell's Defense Against the Dark Arts position.
He learns about forbidden areas at Hogwarts: the forest on the grounds and the third-floor corridor on the right-hand side.
He learns about Quidditch trials and Madam Hooch.
He learns about the school song and the magical portraits in the corridors.
He learns about Peeves, the poltergeist, and his mischievous nature.
He learns the password to the Gryffindor common room is 'Caput Draconis'.
He observes the widespread curiosity and whispering about him from other students.
He learns about the confusing, shifting nature of Hogwarts Castle, including its 142 staircases, moving doors, and walking portraits.
He learns about the mischievous nature of Peeves the Poltergeist and his tricks.
He observes Argus Filch's strictness and his cat, Mrs. Norris, who acts as a spy.
He learns about the curriculum for Astronomy, Herbology (taught by Professor Sprout), and History of Magic (taught by Professor Binns).
He learns about Professor Flitwick's role as the Charms teacher and his excitable nature.
He learns about Professor McGonagall's strict but impressive teaching style in Transfiguration, including her ability to transform objects.
He learns about Professor Quirrell's ineffective teaching of Defense Against the Dark Arts, his garlic-filled classroom, and his dubious stories.
He realizes many other students, like him, come from Muggle families and are new to the wizarding world.
He observes the daily owl post delivery in the Great Hall.
He receives an invitation from Hagrid for tea.
He observes Professor Snape's intense dislike and unfair treatment of him during Potions class.
He learns about the Draught of Living Death (made from asphodel and wormwood), bezoars, and aconite (monkshood/wolfsbane).
He observes Snape's favoritism towards Slytherin students, particularly Malfoy.
He learns about the Gringotts break-in from a Daily Prophet clipping and realizes it happened on his birthday.
He suspects Hagrid knows more about the Gringotts break-in and Snape's dislike than he is letting on.
He observes Malfoy's constant boasting about flying and Quidditch.
He observes Ron's fascination with Quidditch and his attempts to animate Dean's soccer poster.
He observes Neville's extreme nervousness about flying and his accident-prone nature.
He observes Hermione's studious approach to flying, reading 'Quidditch Through the Ages'.
He realizes he has a natural, untaught talent for flying a broomstick.
He learns about the Remembrall, a magical item that glows red when something is forgotten.
He observes Madam Hooch's strict teaching style and her hawk-like yellow eyes.
He feels a rush of joy and confidence from his natural flying ability.
He observes Professor McGonagall's shock and anger at his unauthorized flying.
He learns he is a natural Seeker and is recruited for the Gryffindor Quidditch team.
He learns about the positions in Quidditch, specifically the Seeker, and that first-years are usually not allowed to play.
He learns his father, James Potter, was also an excellent Quidditch player.
He learns about wizard's duels, their rules, and the role of a 'second'.
He realizes Malfoy tricked him about the duel to get him caught by Filch.
He learns the password to the Gryffindor common room is 'Pig snout'.
He learns about the three-headed dog guarding a trapdoor in the forbidden third-floor corridor.
He suspects the three-headed dog is guarding the package from Gringotts vault 713.
He receives a Nimbus Two Thousand broomstick, confirming his place on the Quidditch team.
He learns about the Nimbus Two Thousand broomstick and its superior quality.
He learns the rules of Quidditch, including the roles of Chasers, Keepers, Beaters, and Seekers, and the functions of the Quaffle, Bludgers, and Golden Snitch.
He realizes he has a natural talent for flying and catching.
He learns about the Quidditch stadium and its structure.
He learns that Quidditch games only end when the Snitch is caught, sometimes lasting months.
He observes Professor Flitwick's excitement about teaching flying charms.
He observes Seamus Finnigan's impatience and accidental fire-setting during Charms class.
He observes Hermione Granger's distress and crying in the girls' bathroom.
He observes Professor Quirrell's terrified announcement about the troll.
He observes Percy Weasley's self-important leadership during the troll alert.
He learns about the physical characteristics and behavior of a mountain troll.
He devises a plan to lock the troll in the bathroom.
He devises a plan to distract the troll during the confrontation.
He demonstrates bravery by attacking the troll directly.
He observes Hermione Granger's unexpected lie to protect him and Ron.
He receives five points for Gryffindor for defeating the troll.
He learns about the history of Quidditch fouls, the dangers to Seekers, and the occasional disappearance of referees.
He observes Snape limping and suspects something is wrong with his leg.
He suspects Snape was injured by the three-headed dog on Halloween.
He suspects Snape let the troll in as a diversion to get past the dog.
He feels immense pressure and nervousness before his first Quidditch match.
He observes the Slytherin Captain, Marcus Flint, and believes he looks like he has 'troll blood'.
He experiences his broom being jinxed during the Quidditch match.
He demonstrates exceptional flying skills, even while his broom is jinxed.
He catches the Golden Snitch by almost swallowing it.
He learns the three-headed dog's name is Fluffy.
He learns the guarded item is connected to 'Nicolas Flamel'.
He observes Malfoy's continued unpleasantness and jealousy after the Quidditch match.
He learns that Ron and his brothers are staying at Hogwarts for Christmas because their parents are visiting Charlie in Romania.
He realizes he doesn't feel sorry for himself for staying at Hogwarts for Christmas, anticipating it will be his best Christmas ever.
He observes Hagrid carrying a large fir tree through the corridor.
He expresses hatred for both Malfoy and Snape.
He reveals to Hagrid that he, Ron, and Hermione have been searching for information about Nicolas Flamel.
He learns about the Restricted Section of the library, which contains books on Dark Magic and requires a teacher's note to access.
He observes Madam Pince, the librarian, brandishing a feather duster and being strict.
He receives a roughly cut wooden flute from Hagrid for Christmas.
He receives a fifty-pence piece from Uncle Vernon and Aunt Petunia for Christmas.
He receives a hand-knitted emerald green sweater and homemade fudge from Mrs. Weasley for Christmas.
He receives a large box of Chocolate Frogs from Hermione for Christmas.
He receives and learns about the Invisibility Cloak, a rare and valuable magical item that makes the wearer invisible.
He learns the Invisibility Cloak belonged to his father, James Potter.
He observes Fred and George Weasley wearing their Weasley sweaters.
He observes Percy Weasley's disapproving demeanor and his own Weasley sweater.
He experiences a wizard cracker, which explodes loudly and contains live mice and a hat.
He observes Dumbledore wearing a flowered bonnet and chuckling.
He observes Hagrid kissing Professor McGonagall, who giggles and blushes.
He receives a pack of non-explodable, luminous balloons, a Grow-Your-Own-Warts kit, and a new wizard chess set from wizard crackers.
He realizes he is not good at wizard chess.
He feels a strong desire to use the Invisibility Cloak alone for the first time.
He discovers the Mirror of Erised, an ornate mirror as high as the ceiling.
He sees his parents, Lily and James Potter, and his extended family reflected in the Mirror of Erised for the first time.
He experiences a powerful ache of half joy, half terrible sadness upon seeing his family in the mirror.
He feels an overwhelming desire to return to the Mirror of Erised to see his family again.
He observes Ron's reflection in the Mirror of Erised, showing him as Head Boy, Quidditch Captain, and holding the House and Quidditch Cups.
He learns from Dumbledore that the Mirror of Erised shows 'nothing more or less than the deepest, most desperate desire of our hearts'.
He realizes the Mirror of Erised shows what one wants, not necessarily what is real or possible.
He learns from Dumbledore that 'it does not do to dwell on dreams and forget to live'.
He observes Dumbledore's eccentric response about seeing himself holding socks in the mirror.
He suspects Dumbledore was not entirely truthful about what he saw in the mirror.
He is haunted by nightmares of his parents' death and Voldemort's cackling laughter.
He finds that being tired from Quidditch practice helps reduce his nightmares.
He decides to play the Quidditch match despite Snape refereeing, to avoid appearing scared and to wipe the smiles off Slytherin's faces.
He observes Snape's constant presence and feels Snape might be following him or can read minds.
He feels immense pressure and nervousness before the Quidditch match.
He observes Ron and Hermione's grim and worried expressions before the match.
He feels safe and relieved when Dumbledore arrives to watch the Quidditch match, believing Snape wouldn't dare harm him.
He feels immense happiness and pride after winning the Quidditch match, proving he's more than just a famous name.
He observes Snape's white-faced and tight-lipped reaction to Gryffindor's victory.
He discovers Nicolas Flamel's name on a Dumbledore Chocolate Frog card, linked to alchemy.
He learns about the Sorcerer's Stone, a legendary substance that transforms metal into gold and produces the Elixir of Life, granting immortality.
He learns Nicolas Flamel is 665 years old and lives in Devon with his 658-year-old wife, Perenelle.
He deduces the three-headed dog is guarding Flamel's Sorcerer's Stone, which Dumbledore moved from Gringotts because someone was after it.
He suspects Snape is after the Sorcerer's Stone due to its immense power.
He observes Snape sneaking into the Forbidden Forest after the Quidditch match.
He uses his Invisibility Cloak to follow Snape into the Forbidden Forest.
He overhears a tense conversation between Snape and Quirrell about the Sorcerer's Stone and how to get past Fluffy.
He suspects Quirrell has placed anti-Dark Arts spells guarding the Stone that Snape needs to break through.
He observes Quirrell is getting paler and thinner, but still holding out against Snape.
He gives Quirrell encouraging smiles to bolster his resistance against Snape.
He is looking up 'Dittany' in One Thousand Magical Herbs and Fungi.
He observes Hagrid is hiding something behind his back in the library.
He attempts to ask Hagrid about the Stone's other guardians.
He learns Professor Sprout, Professor Flitwick, Professor McGonagall, Professor Quirrell, and Professor Snape are also guarding the Sorcerer's Stone.
He suspects Snape's involvement in guarding the Stone would make it easy for him to learn how to get past the other teachers' enchantments.
He learns Hagrid owns a huge, black dragon egg.
He observes Ron's fascination with the dragon egg.
He learns the dragon egg is a Norwegian Ridgeback.
He thinks the newly hatched dragon looks like a crumpled, black umbrella.
He observes Hagrid's immediate affection for the baby dragon.
He observes Malfoy looking through the curtains at Hagrid's hut.
He observes Malfoy saw the dragon.
He urges Hagrid to release Norbert.
He suggests sending Norbert to Charlie Weasley in Romania.
He plans to use the Invisibility Cloak to transport Norbert.
He observes Ron's hand is swollen from a dragon bite.
He decides they must risk transporting Norbert despite Malfoy's knowledge.
He observes Fang has a bandaged tail.
He observes Hagrid has been bitten by Norbert.
He helps Hagrid pack Norbert into a crate.
He uses the Invisibility Cloak to transport Norbert through the castle.
He observes Professor McGonagall catching Malfoy out of bed.
He realizes he left the Invisibility Cloak at the top of the tower.
He feels immense shame and guilt for losing Gryffindor 150 points, believing he has ruined their chances for the House Cup.
He observes that his popularity has plummeted, and he is now hated by most students, except Ron.
He resolves not to meddle in things that aren't his business from now on.
He is almost glad for exams, as studying keeps his mind off his misery.
He overhears Quirrell whimpering and sobbing, indicating he is being threatened.
He suspects Snape has just left the classroom where Quirrell was whimpering and that Quirrell has 'given in' to Snape.
He believes they have no proof against Snape, and Dumbledore would not believe them over Snape, especially given their known dislike for him.
He realizes Filch would not help them, as he is friendly with Snape and enjoys students getting expelled.
He realizes they are not supposed to know about the Stone or Fluffy, making any explanation difficult.
He observes Filch's delight at their punishment and his morbid fascination with old, harsh punishments.
He feels relieved when he realizes Hagrid will be supervising their detention.
He observes Malfoy's panic and fear at the prospect of entering the Forbidden Forest.
He learns about unicorn blood shining on the ground and that a unicorn has been badly hurt, the second in a week.
He learns that centaurs are powerful magical creatures and that it's not easy to catch a unicorn.
He observes Hagrid's worry about the injured unicorn.
He suspects the slithering sound they heard was the creature that hurt the unicorn, not a werewolf or another unicorn.
He learns about centaurs, their appearance (man to the waist, horse below), and their sorrowful nature.
He observes Ronan's cryptic responses and his preoccupation with the stars.
He observes Bane's wilder appearance and his anger towards Firenze for interacting with humans.
He learns that centaurs are 'stargazers' and are concerned with what has been foretold in the movements of the planets.
He learns that centaurs are deep thinkers who know things but don't reveal much.
He observes Malfoy's prank on Neville, causing Neville to send up red sparks.
He feels it's his fault Neville is in danger.
He observes the dead unicorn, describing it as beautiful and sad.
He is transfixed by fear when the hooded figure appears.
He experiences an intense pain in his scar, as if it were on fire, when the hooded figure looks at him.
He learns about Firenze, a younger centaur with white-blond hair and a palomino body.
He learns that unicorn blood has the power to keep someone alive, even if they are an inch from death, but at a terrible price: a cursed, half-life.
He learns that only someone with nothing to lose and everything to gain would commit the monstrous crime of slaying a unicorn.
He deduces that someone would drink unicorn blood to stay alive long enough to drink something else that would bring them back to full strength and power, making them immortal.
He realizes the hooded figure drinking unicorn blood is Voldemort, desperately clinging to life.
He learns that centaurs, like Bane, believe in not interfering with destiny as foretold by the planets, even if it means letting Voldemort kill him.
He believes Snape wants the Stone for Voldemort.
He suspects the centaurs' predictions indicate Voldemort's return.
He is still shaking and pacing after his encounter in the forest.
He finds his Invisibility Cloak neatly folded under his sheets with a note from Dumbledore.
He is haunted by nightmares of a hooded figure dripping blood, worse than his previous nightmares.
He realizes he is not as worried about exams as he is about Voldemort and the Stone.
His scar keeps hurting more frequently, which he interprets as a warning of impending danger.
He realizes the stranger who gave Hagrid the dragon egg was likely the one trying to steal the Stone, and that Hagrid inadvertently revealed how to get past Fluffy.
He deduces that Snape is going to try and steal the Stone tonight, now that Dumbledore is gone.
He devises a plan for Hermione to watch Snape and for him and Ron to guard the third-floor corridor.
He declares he will go after the Stone alone if necessary, as Voldemort's return is a greater threat than expulsion.
He plans to use the Invisibility Cloak to get past Neville and then to the trapdoor.
He realizes the Bloody Baron's reputation can be used to scare Peeves away.
He deduces the harp was left by Snape to keep Fluffy asleep, and that it will wake up if the music stops.
He successfully plays Hagrid's flute to keep Fluffy asleep.
He instructs Ron to go to Dumbledore via Hedwig if anything happens to him.
He learns about Devil's Snare and its weakness to light and warmth.
He deduces the winged keys are the next challenge and that they need to catch the correct one.
He demonstrates his natural Seeker abilities by quickly spotting the correct winged key.
He devises a strategy to catch the winged key with Ron and Hermione.
He learns that the next chamber is a giant chessboard where they must play their way across.
He understands Ron's plan to sacrifice himself in the chess game.
He observes a troll, larger than the one they previously fought, already knocked out.
He learns the next challenge is a potion puzzle set by Snape.
He realizes there is only enough potion for one person to go forward.
He decides to go forward alone, telling Hermione to go back for Ron and Dumbledore.
He uses his scar as a symbol of his past luck and hope for future survival.
He learns that Professor Quirrell was possessed by Voldemort and was the true antagonist trying to steal the Sorcerer's Stone.
He learns that Snape was actually trying to protect him during the Quidditch match and was not trying to kill him.
He learns that Snape hated his father, James Potter, but was in his debt because James saved Snape's life.
He learns that the Mirror of Erised was enchanted by Dumbledore to give the Stone only to someone who wanted to find it, not use it.
He learns that the Sorcerer's Stone has been destroyed.
He learns that Nicolas Flamel and his wife will die now that the Stone is destroyed, but Dumbledore views death as 'the next great adventure'.
He learns from Dumbledore that 'fear of a name increases fear of the thing itself' and should call Voldemort by his name.
He learns that his mother's love, a powerful magic, left a protective mark on him that Voldemort cannot understand or touch.
He learns that his Invisibility Cloak belonged to his father, James Potter, who used it for sneaking around.
He receives a photo album filled with pictures of his parents and their friends from Hagrid.
He receives sixty points for Gryffindor for 'pure nerve and outstanding courage'.
He observes the entire school knows about his adventure with Quirrell.
He plans to use the rule against magic outside of school to torment Dudley during the summer.
He knows the tabby cat is Professor McGonagall in animagus form.
He uses the Put-Outer to extinguish streetlights, ensuring secrecy.
He confirms Voldemort's disappearance and the death of Lily and James Potter.
He understands Harry's miraculous survival and the significance of his scar.
He believes placing Harry with the Dursleys is the best course of action for his future.
He implicitly trusts Hagrid with important tasks, despite his occasional carelessness.
He orchestrated Hagrid's mission to deliver Harry's letter and bring him to the magical world.
He knows the rules and traditions of Hogwarts, including forbidden areas.
He deliberately uses cryptic and humorous language in his opening speech.
He can magically conjure a ribbon with the school song lyrics from his wand.
He observes Harry's presence in the Mirror of Erised room, despite Harry being invisible.
He reveals he doesn't need a cloak to become invisible, implying other magical means.
He knows the Mirror of Erised shows 'nothing more or less than the deepest, most desperate desire of our hearts'.
He understands the dangers of the Mirror of Erised, having seen others waste away before it.
He advises Harry not to dwell on dreams and forget to live.
He gives an eccentric and possibly untruthful answer about seeing himself with socks in the mirror.
He convinced Harry not to seek out the Mirror of Erised again.
He is present at the Quidditch match, observing the game and Harry.
He congratulates Harry on his Quidditch victory and subtly references Harry's experience with the Mirror of Erised.
He is famous for defeating Grindelwald in 1945, discovering the twelve uses of dragon's blood, and his work on alchemy with Nicolas Flamel.
He is one of the teachers who helped enchant the Sorcerer's Stone's defenses.
He leaves a cryptic note with the Invisibility Cloak, 'Just in case'.
He leaves Hogwarts after receiving an urgent owl from the Ministry of Magic, creating an opportunity for the Stone to be stolen.
He knows Quirrell was possessed by Voldemort.
He reveals Snape was protecting Harry, not harming him, during the Quidditch match.
He reveals the Sorcerer's Stone has been destroyed.
He advises Harry that 'fear of a name increases fear of the thing itself' and to use Voldemort's name.
He explains that Harry's mother's love left a protective mark on him that Voldemort cannot touch.
He reveals James Potter saved Snape's life, creating a debt Snape couldn't bear.
He explains he enchanted the Mirror of Erised to give the Stone only to someone who wanted to find it, not use it.
He observes the entire school knows about Harry's adventure.
He awards points to Gryffindor for Ron, Hermione, Harry, and Neville's bravery.
He believes 'death is but the next great adventure' for a well-organized mind.
She observes the Dursleys' 'unDursleyish' behavior and their son's spoiled nature.
She notes the widespread magical celebrations and Muggle awareness of strange occurrences.
She is aware of the rumors surrounding Voldemort's disappearance and the Potters' fate.
She expresses strong disapproval of leaving Harry with the Dursleys due to their character.
She worries about Harry's future fame and the impact it will have on him.
She knows the rules and traditions of the Sorting Ceremony and the Hogwarts Houses.
She observes Neville's disheveled cloak and Ron's smudged nose, indicating their need to smarten up.
She demonstrates advanced Transfiguration skills by changing her desk into a pig and back.
She teaches the fundamental principles of Transfiguration, emphasizing its complexity and danger.
She observes Hermione's successful Transfiguration attempt and gives her a rare smile of approval.
She quickly spots trouble brewing between Malfoy and Neville.
She demonstrates strict authority during flying lessons, forbidding students from moving their brooms.
She is almost speechless with shock and anger at Harry's reckless flying.
She recognizes Harry's natural talent for flying, comparing him to Charlie Weasley.
She knows the rules regarding first-years and Quidditch, but is willing to bend them for Harry.

Her family's secret fear of the Potters being discovered.

S-GBY-001.summary: On a seemingly ordinary Tuesday morning, the Dursleys, a family obsessed with their own normalcy, begin their day. Mr. Dursley hums, Mrs. Dursley gossips, and Dudley throws a tantrum, all while an unnoticed tawny owl flutters past their window. The narrator reveals their deepest fear: the secret of their connection to the 'unDursleyish' Potters and their son.

knowledgeMutations.content: The observation of a cat behaving unnaturally (reading a map, then a sign).

S-GBY-002.summary: Mr. Dursley leaves for work, his mind on drills, but his routine is disrupted by the sight of a cat reading a map. He dismisses it as a trick of light, but the cat continues to observe him, its behavior increasingly peculiar. This initial encounter plants a seed of unease in his perfectly normal day.

knowledgeMutations.content: He overhears whispers about 'The Potters' and 'their son, Harry' from the cloaked people.
He is called a 'Muggle' by a stranger, a term he doesn't understand.
He learns of widespread unusual animal behavior (owls, shooting stars) from the news.
She learns of the strange news reports from Mr. Dursley.

relationshipMutations.type: Growing tension and fear over shared secret

S-GBY-003.summary: Throughout his workday, Mr. Dursley is increasingly unnerved by the proliferation of strangely dressed people in cloaks, whispering excitedly. He overhears the name 'Potter' and 'Harry' and is gripped by fear, attempting to call his wife before dismissing his worries. Later, an old man in a violet cloak hugs him, proclaiming 'You-Know-Who has gone' and calling him a 'Muggle,' further shattering his sense of normalcy. At home, a news report confirms widespread unusual animal behavior and shooting stars, solidifying his growing dread.

knowledgeMutations.content: She confirms her nephew's name is Harry.

relationshipMutations.type: Shared anxiety over the 'Potter problem'

Mrs. Dursley, though angry, confirms their nephew's name is Harry. A screaming book alerts Filch and Snape, forcing Harry to hide in an unused classroom, where he discovers the Mirror of Erised. He returns with Ron the next night, but Ron sees himself as Head Boy and Quidditch Captain, leading to an argument. Dumbledore then appears, explaining the Mirror's true nature: it shows the heart's deepest desires, and warns Harry not to dwell on dreams and forget to live.

S-GBY-067.summary: Harry attends the end-of-year feast, initially seeing the Great Hall decked in Slytherin colors. Dumbledore, in his usual eccentric style, begins the House Cup ceremony, announcing Slytherin as the winner. However, he then awards last-minute points for bravery: fifty to Ron for chess, fifty to Hermione for logic, sixty to Harry for courage, and ten to Neville for standing up to his friends. This dramatic turn of events causes the Great Hall to transform into Gryffindor colors, securing their victory in the House Cup. Snape is forced to shake McGonagall's hand with a forced smile, confirming his continued animosity towards Harry.

S-GBY-068.summary: After passing their exams, Harry, Ron, and Hermione board the Hogwarts Express for the journey home. They reflect on the year, and Harry receives a warning about using magic over the holidays. At King's Cross, they pass through the barrier to the Muggle world, where Ginny Weasley points out Harry. Mrs. Weasley greets Harry warmly, but the Dursleys arrive, looking terrified at Harry's presence. Uncle Vernon rudely rushes Harry away, but Harry, with a grin, reveals to Ron and Hermione his plan to torment Dudley during the summer, knowing the Dursleys are unaware of the rule against magic outside of school.

worldSummary: The Dursleys, obsessed with normalcy, are increasingly disturbed by strange occurrences and whispers of the Potters, whom they despise. Meanwhile, Albus Dumbledore and Minerva McGonagall arrive on Privet Drive, revealing the death of Lily and James Potter and the miraculous survival of their son, Harry, at the hands of Voldemort. They prepare to leave baby Harry on the Dursleys' doorstep, setting the stage for his unusual upbringing. Ten years after Harry Potter was left on their doorstep, the Dursleys continue their abusive treatment of him, forcing him to live in a cupboard while doting on their spoiled son, Dudley. On Dudley's birthday, a series of strange events at the zoo, culminating in a boa constrictor's magical escape, further solidifies the Dursleys' fear and hatred of Harry's inexplicable abilities, leading to his severe punishment and deepening his isolation. Harry endures the Dursleys' continued abuse and attempts to suppress his burgeoning magical identity, culminating in a relentless barrage of mysterious letters addressed to him. Uncle Vernon's increasingly desperate and comical efforts to evade these letters lead the family on a frantic escape, only to find themselves stranded in a remote shack as Harry's eleventh birthday approaches, with an ominous knock at the door. On Harry's eleventh birthday, Hagrid, a giant from the magical world, arrives at the Dursleys' remote shack, revealing Harry's true identity as a wizard and delivering his Hogwarts acceptance letter. Hagrid confronts the Dursleys about their abuse and deception, exposing the truth about Harry's parents' deaths and Voldemort's attack, forever changing Harry's understanding of himself and his past. Harry, now aware of his wizard identity, embarks on a journey with Hagrid to London to prepare for Hogwarts. He discovers his parents left him a fortune at Gringotts, experiences the magical world of Diagon Alley, and has a pivotal encounter with his future wand, which shares a connection to Voldemort. This chapter solidifies Harry's entry into the wizarding world and hints at his unique destiny. Harry spends his last miserable month with the Dursleys, who are now terrified of him, before they reluctantly take him to King's Cross. There, he discovers the magical Platform Nine and Three-Quarters, where he meets the boisterous Weasley family and begins his journey to Hogwarts, forming his first friendships and encountering new magical wonders and challenges. Harry and the other first-years arrive at Hogwarts, are introduced to Professor McGonagall and the concept of the Hogwarts Houses, and witness the Sorting Ceremony. Harry is sorted into Gryffindor, makes new friends, and experiences the magical feast, but a mysterious pain from a new teacher hints at lurking dangers. Harry navigates his first week at Hogwarts, struggling with the castle's confusing layout and the eccentricities of his professors. He develops a strong dislike for Professor Snape, who unfairly targets him in Potions class, but finds solace in his growing friendship with Ron and a tea invitation from Hagrid. A newspaper clipping at Hagrid's hut hints at a connection between the Gringotts break-in and the mysterious package Hagrid retrieved, deepening the mystery surrounding Harry's past and future. Harry's first flying lesson at Hogwarts takes a dramatic turn when Neville's accident leads to Malfoy stealing his Remembrall. Harry's natural flying talent is revealed when he retrieves it, earning him a spot on the Gryffindor Quidditch team. Later, Malfoy challenges Harry to a midnight wizard's duel, which inadvertently leads Harry, Ron, and Hermione to discover a monstrous three-headed dog guarding a trapdoor in a forbidden corridor. Harry and Ron revel in their newfound friendship and Harry's Quidditch success, while Hermione's continued ostracization leads to a dangerous encounter with a troll. The trio's unexpected teamwork against the troll forges a powerful bond, marking the true beginning of their friendship and revealing Snape's suspicious behavior. As Harry prepares for his first Quidditch match, he, Ron, and Hermione uncover suspicious activity from Snape, leading them to believe he is after the item guarded by the three-headed dog. During the match, Harry's broom is jinxed, but Hermione's quick thinking saves him, allowing Gryffindor to win. The trio confronts Hagrid, who accidentally reveals the name 'Nicolas Flamel,' deepening the mystery. As Christmas approaches, Harry, Ron, and Hermione continue their quest to identify Nicolas Flamel, leading Harry to discover the mysterious Mirror of Erised, which reveals his deepest desire: his family. Dumbledore intervenes, explaining the mirror's dangers and revealing its true nature, while Harry grapples with the emotional impact of seeing his parents for the first time. Harry struggles with nightmares after seeing his parents in the Mirror of Erised, while the trio continues their search for Nicolas Flamel. Snape's unexpected appointment as Quidditch referee raises suspicions, leading to a tense match. Harry's victory is overshadowed by his discovery of a secret meeting between Snape and Quirrell in the Forbidden Forest, confirming their fears about the Sorcerer's Stone. Harry, Ron, and Hermione continue to unravel the mystery of the Sorcerer's Stone, learning more about its guardians and Hagrid's dangerous secret: a dragon egg. Their efforts to help Hagrid hatch and then secretly rehome the dragon, Norbert, lead to a series of escalating mishaps, culminating in Malfoy discovering their illicit activities and the trio losing the Invisibility Cloak, putting them in grave danger of detention. After being caught out of bed, Harry, Hermione, and Neville face a harsh detention in the Forbidden Forest, where they encounter injured unicorns and mysterious centaurs. Harry then confronts a hooded figure drinking unicorn blood, revealing Voldemort's desperate attempt to survive. This terrifying experience solidifies Harry's understanding of the danger surrounding the Sorcerer's Stone and forces him to accept his role in the unfolding conflict. Harry, Ron, and Hermione navigate their final exams under the shadow of Voldemort's potential return, with Harry's scar burning as a constant warning. A sudden realization about Hagrid's dragon egg leads them to uncover how the stranger learned to get past Fluffy, prompting a desperate attempt to warn Dumbledore. When Dumbledore is absent, the trio takes matters into their own hands, braving the trapdoor and the first magical defenses, including Devil's Snare, a bewitched chess game, and a troll, before Hermione sacrifices herself to allow Harry to proceed alone. Harry awakens in the hospital wing to Dumbledore's presence, learning the truth about Quirrell's possession by Voldemort and Snape's protective actions. Dumbledore reveals the Sorcerer's Stone has been destroyed, ensuring Voldemort's continued weakness, and explains the power of Harry's mother's love. Harry, Ron, and Hermione celebrate Gryffindor's unexpected House Cup victory at the end-of-year feast, and Harry returns to the Dursleys, now armed with the knowledge of his magical identity and a plan to torment Dudley.
